title: Table: The table compares the international tourist arrivals in millions in 9 countries...
description: The table compares the international tourist arrivals in millions in 9 countries in 2009 and 2010 and the changes (in percentages). Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT NHANH

1. Hiểu đề

Bảng so sánh lượng khách du lịch quốc tế (triệu người) ở 9 quốc gia trong 2009 vs 2010, kèm % thay đổi

2. Introduction

The table compares international tourist arrivals in nine countries in 2009 and 2010, along with the percentage changes.

3. Overview

Hầu hết các quốc gia tăng nhẹ, ngoại trừ Hong Kong giảm; France vẫn cao nhất và không đổi, trong khi Germany và China có tăng trưởng mạnh nhất

4. Body

Body 1 (Top \+ stable \+ small growth):

France giữ nguyên (76); US, Spain, UK, Italy tăng nhẹ (khoảng \+1–3%), trong đó UK tăng nhiều nhất trong nhóm nhỏ (+3.6%)

Body 2 (Big changes):

Germany tăng mạnh nhất (11 → 23, \+109%); China (22 → 34, \+55%) và Turkey (33 → 45, \+33%) cũng tăng đáng kể; Hong Kong là nước duy nhất giảm (10 → 9, \-11%)

5. Key phrases

remained unchanged; rose slightly; saw a sharp increase; experienced the fastest growth; the only decline

The table compares the number of international tourist arrivals, in millions, in nine countries in 2009 and 2010, along with the percentage change over the period.

Overall, France and the US received the highest numbers of tourists in both years, while most countries experienced growth, particularly Germany and China. In contrast, Hong Kong was the only country to record a decline.

In detail, France remained the most visited country, with arrivals unchanged at 76 million in both years. The US ranked second, rising slightly from 66 to 67 million. Spain and the UK had similar figures in 2009 (both 55 million), but the UK grew more noticeably to 57 million, compared to Spain’s modest increase to 56 million. Italy also saw a small rise from 44 to 45 million.

More substantial growth was observed in several countries. Turkey increased sharply from 33 to 45 million, while China rose significantly from 22 to 34 million. The most dramatic change occurred in Germany, where arrivals more than doubled from 11 to 23 million. By contrast, Hong Kong was the only exception, with a slight decrease from 10 to 9 million.
